messages.content:
Rewrite in the style of the King James Bible.

" Maynard woke slowly and was surprised to see that the three experts had increased to ten.
     They began to fire questions at him before he had fully reoriented himself.
     "How big was this vessel? Describe, if you can, the appearance of the stars."
     Even as he answered, he could hear others talking quickly: "Cisterine, undoubtedly, it explains the additional stocks of Cuderium, no wonder shares fell by twelve points. Our friends have been mining it wholesale."
     "How did they get past the patrols?"
     "Your guess is as good as mine. What riles me, however, is the fact that they had to play God to pass the time."
     Another, rather ageing voice, said: "Oh, indeed, no, not merely to pass the time, take a look at this — "
     One of his original questioners came over. "Well, Maynard, undoubtedly, you have blown the roof off something as you can see. In the meantime, a few words in private, this way, please.""

And lo, Maynard awakened from his slumber, yet his eyes beheld a great astonishment, for the three experts had multiplied in number and become ten.

Swiftly they began to assail him with queries, ere he could fully regain his bearings. "How vast was this vessel? Describe, if thou art able, the countenance of the stars," they demanded.

Even as he delivered his response, he could overhear others conversing briskly amongst themselves. "Surely 'tis Cisterine," said one, "for it doth elucidate the augmented stocks of Cuderium. No marvel that shares plummeted by twelve points. Verily, our companions have extracted it in abundance."

"How did they elude the diligent patrols?" queried another. "Thy conjecture is equal to mine. Yet what vexeth me is the truth that they deemed themselves as gods in their leisure."

Then spake forth a voice, aged and wise: "Nay, not only to pass time, mark my words—behold this marvel that I present to thee—"

And lo, one of the original interrogators approached Maynard, saying, "Truly, thy revelations hath rent the firmament asunder. But for now, a few private words, I beseech thee, step this way."

And so it came to pass that Maynard awakened from his slumber.
And lo, he was greatly surprised to see that the three experts had multiplied in number, and now there were ten of them.
And without delay, they began to bombard him with questions, before he could fully orient himself.
And they inquired, "How grand was this vessel? Canst thou describe the appearance of the majestic stars?"
And even as Maynard sought to provide an answer, he heard others conversing hastily among themselves.
And one among them declared, "Surely it is Cisterine, which doth explain the augmented stocks of Cuderium. No wonder the shares have plummeted by twelve points. Verily, our acquaintances have been mining it abundantly."
And another questioned, "But how did they manage to surpass the diligent patrols?"
And replied another, "Thy guess is as good as mine. Nonetheless, what angers me is the reality that they assumed godlike roles to amuse themselves."
And then, a voice, elderly and wise, interjected, saying, "Oh, but it is more than mere amusement. Behold this extraordinary evidence—"
And approaching Maynard once more was one of his original inquirers, who spoke thus, "Indeed, Maynard, thou hast caused a great upheaval with thy revelations. Yet for now, I beseech thee, come aside with me for a few private words."

And thus it befell that Maynard gradually stirred from his slumber, his eyes alighting upon a sight both bewildering and magnificent, for the trio of experts had multiplied in number, swelling their ranks to a congregation of ten.

With rapidity unmatched, they unleashed a torrent of inquiries upon Maynard, as if impelled by an unseen force, ere he could fully regain his bearings in the ethereal realm of wakefulness.

"How vast and resplendent was this celestial vessel thou beheld? Pray, if thou canst, grant our longing minds a portrayal of the celestial tapestry bedecked with stars," they beseeched in unison, their voices intertwining like strands of gilded thread.

Amidst Maynard's response, a cascade of whispered discourse came to his ears, as if the very air were abuzz with murmurs of subsurface revelations. "Surely, it is the coveted Cisterine that fashions the key, unlocking the enigma of augmented stocks of Cuderium. No wonder the shares plummeted by twelve points. In verity, our compatriots have delved into its depths with unrestrained fervor."

"And how, in the name of celestial guardians, did they elude the vigilant patrols assigned to the skies?" queried one, his voice tinged with bewilderment. "Thy supposition is as valid as mine own. Yet it doth rouse my ire that they dared to assume divine prerogative for their own amusement," came the impassioned response, resonating with indignant reverberations.

Then, in a grand proclamation, a voice, laden with the wisdom of ages, interjected, proclaiming, "Nay, it transpires that their purpose transcended mere idleness. Come hither and behold this extraordinary revelation I bring forth akin to a radiant phoenix rising from the ashes—"

Drawing near to Maynard once more was the original inquirer, his countenance grave and solemn. "Assuredly, Maynard, thou hast rent asunder the very fabric of existence with thy profound revelations. Yet, I implore thee, accompany me forthwith, to a sanctuary of whispered confidences, where we may exchange thoughts in utmost secrecy."

Emerged from slumber, Maynard beheld a wondrous sight: the three experts had multiplied, their number swelling to ten.

Without pause, they inundated him with swift and ceaseless queries, ere he could fully reorient himself.

"Describe the vastness of the vessel! Reveal the splendor of the stars!" they clamored with fervor.

Amidst their eager voices, Maynard caught whispers of revelation. "Cisterine, the key to dwindling shares of Cuderium! They have mined it with unbounded zeal!"

"How evaded the vigilant patrols?" asked one, vexed. Another responded, anger lacing his words, "A godlike game to pass time!"

Then a voice, ancient and knowing, intoned, "Nay, 'tis no idle pastime! Witness this prodigious proof—"

Drawing near, an original inquirer beckoned Maynard, imploring his presence in private. "Thou hast unveiled cataclysmic truth! Come, speak behind guarded walls."
Make it follow the Subject-Object-Verb format.

Maynard, from his slumber, gradually woke, and to his great surprise, he beheld an increase from three to ten experts.

With haste, they unleashed upon him a flurry of questions, before his full reorientation had been achieved.

"How big was this vessel? Canst thou describe the appearance of the stars?" they asked.

As he answered, he could hear others speaking swiftly, saying, "Undoubtedly, it is Cisterine, accounting for the augmented stocks of Cuderium. No wonder shares fell by twelve points. Our companions have mined it wholesale."

"How did they pass the patrols?" inquired one. "Thy guess is as good as mine. What vexes me, however, is their audacity to play God in their leisure," lamented another.

An older voice added, "Indeed, not merely for amusement, behold this evidence—"

Approaching him, one of the original questioners said, "Maynard, thou hast surely unearthed a matter of great magnitude. Pray, a few private words hence, if thou please."

From the depths of his slumber did Maynard slowly awaken, and behold! He was struck with sheer astonishment, for the humble trio of experts had multiplied exponentially, expanding their ranks to a grand total of ten.

Swiftly and without respite, they unleashed upon him a veritable deluge of queries, ere his faculties of comprehension could be fully restored.

"Pray, recount the magnitude of this celestial vessel! Dost thou possess the words to depict the ethereal countenance of the resplendent stars?" they ardently demanded.

Even as Maynard endeavored to provide his response, he found himself ensnared within a charmed web of whispers and fervent discourse that danced upon the very air. "Mayhap it is the fabled Cisterine that unravels the perplexing enigma, unraveling the ever-volatile stocks of Cuderium. Oh, the shares, how they descended steeply, faltering by a formidable dozen points. Verily, our faithful companions have embarked upon wholesale extraction of this precious resource."

"How, we beseech thee, did they succeed in evading the ever-vigilant patrols?" queried one, his tone tinged with vexation. To which another retorted, indignation dripping from his words, "Thine conjecture holds equal weight with mine. Yet what stirs the fires of my ire is the stark reality that they dared to assume the mantle of deities, partaking in a cacophony of divine amusement."

Thereafter, a voice of age, steeped in wisdom accrued through the eras, interposed with solemnity, announcing, "Oh, but it surpasses mere dalliance and revelry. Cast thine gaze upon this extraordinary harbinger—a revelation that shall set thy world ablaze!"

Drawing closer, one among the original inquirers beckoned Maynard, his visage marked by gravity and hushed intention. "Undoubtedly, dear Maynard, thou hast unleashed a cataclysmic torrent with thy profound revelations. Therefore, I implore thee, let us withdraw to a sanctuary veiled in secrecy, where we may exchange thoughts in utmost confidence and guarded whispers."

Startled from slumber, Maynard beheld a tenfold increase in the number of experts before him.

Promptly, they bombarded him with questions, before he fully regained his bearings.

"What was the size of the vessel? Describe the starry appearance," they pressed.

Amidst his replies, Maynard overheard others speaking urgently. "Undoubtedly Cisterine, explaining the dwindling Cuderium shares. Our allies mined it extensively."

"How did they elude patrols?" one queried. "Thy guess is mine, but their godlike pastime irks me," lamented another.

An aged voice interjected, "Nay, 'tis more than mere pastime. Behold this evidence."

Approaching Maynard, an original questioner requested, "Thou hast uncovered something momentous. In private, a few words, if thou please."
Make the text follow the Subject-Object-Verb format but keep the condensed baroque writing.

Maynard, awakened from slumber, beheld with astonishment the tenfold increase of experts before him.

Without delay, they inundated him with questions, ere his full reorientation he achieved.

"What size had this vessel? Canst thou describe stars' appearance?" they implored.

As he replied, whispers could be heard amid urgent discourse: "Undoubtedly Cisterine, stocks of Cuderium explained it doth. Shares fell twelve points, for wholesale mining by our companions."

"How passed they patrols?" queried one. "Thy guess equal mine. Yet their godlike play for time rankles me," another lamented.

An aged voice interjected, "Nay, beyond mere amusement, behold this evidence—"

Approaching Maynard, an original inquirer beckoned, entreating, "Maynard, momentous revelation brought forth by thee. In private, a few words, if thou please."
Keep the changes, but rewrite it in Yoda speak.

Awakened from slumber, Maynard was, and lo! Before him, the number of the experts had multiplied tenfold.

Questions, without delay, they poured upon him, ere his reorientation complete, he could become.

"What size had this vessel? Canst thou describe appearance of the stars?" they implored.

Even as he answered, whispers among urgent discourse were heard: "Cisterine, undoubtedly, it explains the stocks of Cuderium, fallen twelve points. Mined it wholesale, our companions have."

"How passed they patrols?" queried one. "Thy guess is mine; however, their godlike play for time, it irks me," lamented another.

An aged voice interjected, "Nay, mere amusement it surpasses; behold this evidence—"

Approaching Maynard, one of the original inquirers came forth, entreating, "Maynard, momentous revelation brought forth by thee, it has. In private, a few words, if thou please."
